title: The error term in the prime number theorem

thm:1.summary: Explicit PNT error term: with $R=5.573412$, $\left|\frac{\psi(x)-x}{x}\right| \le A\left(\frac{\log x}{R}\right)^B \exp\!\left(-C\sqrt{\frac{\log x}{R}}\right)$ and $|\psi(x)-x|\le \epsilon_0 x$ for $\log x \ge X$ over a table of $(X,A,B,C,\epsilon_0)$; asymptotically $B,C\to 2$ and $A\to 192$.

cor:1.summary: The same explicit error bound transferred to $\theta(x)$: $\left|\frac{\theta(x)-x}{x}\right| \le A_1(\frac{\log x}{R})^B \exp(-C\sqrt{\log x/R})$ with $A_1 = A + 0.1$.

cor:2.summary: Explicit bound on the prime-counting error: $|\pi(x)-\mathrm{li}(x)| \le 235\,x(\log x)^{0.52}\exp(-0.8\sqrt{\log x})$ for $\log x \ge 2000$.

thm:2.summary: Application to Ramanujan's inequality: $\pi^2(x) < \frac{e x}{\log x}\pi(x/e)$ holds for $38\,358\,837\,682 < x \le \exp(58)$ and for $x \ge \exp(3915)$.

lem:1.summary: Truncated explicit formula: for $50<T<x$, $x>e^{60}$ half an odd integer, $\frac{\psi(x)-x}{x} = -\sum_{|\gamma|<T}\frac{x^{\rho-1}}{\rho} + \vartheta\!\left(\frac{2\log^2 x}{T}\right)$ — the reduction of the error term to a finite sum over zeros.

lem:2.summary: Sum over ordinates of zeros: for $T \ge 2\pi e$, $\sum_{0<\gamma\le T}\frac{1}{\gamma} = \frac{1}{4\pi}(\log\frac{T}{2\pi})^2 + \vartheta(0.9321)$.

lem:3.summary: The zero-free region used: no zeros of $\zeta(s)$ in $\sigma \ge 1 - 1/(R\log t)$ for $t\ge 3$ with $R=5.573412$ — the classical-type input whose constant directly sets $R$ in Theorem 1.

lem:4.summary: Verified Riemann Hypothesis up to height $3\times 10^{12}$: every zero $\beta+i\gamma$ with $|\gamma|\le 3\times 10^{12}$ has $\beta=\tfrac12$ (rigorous computation of Platt).

lem:5.summary: Zero-density estimate (Pintz's method): for fixed $\sigma\in[0.75,1)$, $N(\sigma,T) \le C_1(\sigma)T^{8(1-\sigma)/3}\log^{5-2\sigma}T + C_2(\sigma)\log^2 T$ — bounds the count of off-line zeros that the explicit-formula sum must absorb.

lem:6.summary: Explicit $\theta$ bound in a finite range: for $x\in(599,\exp(58)]$, $|\theta(x)-x| \le \frac{\sqrt{x}}{8\pi}\log^2 x$; follows from Büthe's Theorem 2 coupled with the verified-RH Lemma 4.

edges.note: The error term is obtained from the truncated explicit formula (Lemma 1).
The constant $R=5.573412$ in Theorem 1 is exactly the zero-free-region constant of Lemma 3.
The off-line zeros in the explicit-formula sum are controlled by the zero-density estimate (Lemma 5).
Zeros below height $3\times 10^{12}$ are handled by the verified RH (Lemma 4).
Evaluating and truncating the sum over zeros uses the estimate for $\sum_{0<\gamma\le T}1/\gamma$ (Lemma 2).
The $\theta$ error bound is transferred from the $\psi$ error bound (Theorem 1).
The $\pi(x)-\mathrm{li}(x)$ bound follows from the $\psi$/$\theta$ error bounds.
The Ramanujan-inequality application uses the explicit error term (Theorem 1).
The finite-range part of the Ramanujan inequality uses the explicit $\theta$ bound (Lemma 6).
The explicit $\theta$ bound is coupled with the verified RH (Lemma 4).

main_result.description: The explicit PNT error term for $\psi$ and its transfer to $\theta$ and $\pi$.

explicit_formula.description: The truncated explicit formula reducing the error to a finite sum over zeros, and the sum-over-ordinates estimate.

zero_inputs.description: The three inputs that control the zeros: the zero-free region (setting $R$), the verified RH up to height $3\times 10^{12}$, and the Pintz zero-density estimate.

application.description: Application to Ramanujan's $\pi^2(x)$ inequality, using the error term and an explicit finite-range $\theta$ bound.

Platt.title: D. Platt — rigorous computation of zeros of $\zeta$

Dusart.title: P. Dusart — explicit estimates for prime functions

Booth2.title: Büthe — explicit estimates under partial RH
